transcript.text: Yeah, so I guess before I get started, I'm also curious, like, is the idea to develop an understanding of the platform's, like, I guess, value and use cases for your other clients?
Or is it more to, like, help drive, like, the content ideas for HockeyStack?
Yeah, so we use it as kind of input into our workflows to kind of create content.
So it's definitely, like, this product, Deep Dive, helps us inform different artifacts that we create that will then flow into how we create content for you guys that targets your ICPs.
Cool.
Okay.
Yeah, that works for me.
So, yeah, in that case, I can definitely just give you, like, the high level and some of the most common use cases.
But like you mentioned, let's keep it conversational.
If any specific questions do come up, I'm always happy to take a quick pause and dive into anything.
Yeah, absolutely.
Cool.
Awesome.
I'll share my screen here.
So the biggest challenge that we see in go-to-market today typically comes from just the amount of...

of sheer data ingestion over time, because we're working with a lot of, you know, scale-ups, but also a lot of, like, enterprise companies that are collecting a ton of data from a ton of different sources, it gets increasingly harder to extract insights from that data, and so that's why teams need not only a way to collect the data, but then also, like, normalize it, categorize it, and then map it out onto a single-buyer journey, so you can kind of understand, like, what touchpoints are influencing down-funnel metrics, like, pipeline revenue, that type of thing.

And so, because the B2B buyer journey is so complex, the problem of data ingestion becomes really prevalent when you're trying to make decisions about what, like, what channels, what campaigns, what types of strategies to focus on.
We actually ran a report that shows, it takes, like, hundreds of impressions and dozens of touchpoints for a deal to go close to one.
And so, with, like, standard CRM reporting, or, like, traditional attribution modeling, teams are going to see that, like, 80% of

Deals are coming from like direct or organic, but that's just because if they have a last touch model, it's only taking that last touch into account, obviously, and it's not taking into account any of the other touch points, especially the top of funnel brand awareness stuff that has kind of impacted that deal to get created.
And so that's what we're going to be able to do at HockeyStack is track every single touch point from the very first anonymous impression all the way through to closed one.
And I'll kind of dive into like what that looks like as well.
Other tools are going to be able to show you like what's working, what's not at a high level.
But the problem is that they can be inefficient for a couple of ways.

The first is that because every platform out there tracks data differently, unless you like get everything into one buyer journey timeline and line everything up, you're not going to be able to like extract the insights just because those touch points aren't going to like line up, especially with like the CRI modeling with needing to have, you know, contact assigned to an opportunity.
So that's why we have Odin, our AI analyst, where you can actually ask any type of real world business question like, hey, we have $10,000 of marketing budget remaining for this quarter.
What should we focus on?
Or, you know, high level like, hey, which marketing channels are generating the highest quality leads consistently?

So Odin would be able to kind of eliminate the process that it normally takes of creating dashboards, reports, defining KPIs, putting attribution models and everything, and then still manually analyzing all that data.
So our philosophy is more around, rather than just proving past ROI, it's around like, how do we use this data to make decisions for future strategies in order to like create future value?
Gotcha.
And the way that this works is we're going to connect to any platform that helps tell the story of the buyer journeys.

So typically our customers are going to be using Salesforce or HubSpot, so we'll connect with our CRM, and then we'll connect with whatever marketing automation platform they're using, so Pardot, Marketo, HubSpot, and then we connect natively with all the paid channels as well.
So whether it's LinkedIn, Google, Meta, Reddit, TikTok, any of the big ones, we'll be able to ingest data from there as well through APIs.
And then we'll put a tracking pixel on their website.
So this is how we're going be able to not only understand the activity from the converted users that already exist in their CRM, but also all of the anonymous users, right?

Because, you know, typically if a company is evaluating a software platform, they're going to have like four or five people from that company looking at different pieces of content, different web pages, but only one person is going to fill out a form and convert, right?
The rest of them are going to probably avoid a form fill like the plague, just to avoid like email lists and things like that.
So that's how HockeyStack is going to be valuable on that side, is like bringing in additional data points from that anonymous activity.
Okay.
We're also going to be able to actually bring in, like, third-party signals.
So we have AI agents that would be able to find, like, you know, hiring signals, funding rounds, news articles, job changes, anything like that.
And that's all customizable.
And then that'll kind of feed into our account intelligence side.
But, like, what we're going to do is we'll create an AI account context on every account, which is going to be the backbone for both our products.
So we have the marketing intelligence, which is, like, attribution.
You know, we have templated approaches and custom reports through our report builder UI in the platform.
We also have, like, lift and incrementality reporting as a part of that custom reporting.
And then Odin, our AI analyst, where you can ask those real-world business questions.

But then we also have the account intelligence side, which is going to be more around, like, bringing in that same data set and then surfacing it to sales in the form of intent signals and account scoring so they can build all their priority lists.
And then we can also automate workflows to capitalize on that signal data at scale.
And then we have another AI agent on that side called Nova, where leadership...
He's going to be able to go in and ask questions about, like, you know, how are my best reps performing?
Like, what are they doing differently than, like, you know, the rest of the team?
Or they can ask specific questions about, like, segments, geographic regions, or also specific accounts as well.
And I'll show you what that looks like.
But that's kind of more on the side of, like, how do we take this data and then craft a plan to break into a specific account as well?
Gotcha.
So would you say the, yeah, would you say the account context, that is split between, like, sales and marketing?
You have both sides of the kind of coin there for go-to-market?
Yeah, absolutely.
Okay.
And that comes down to both our products.
But I'll kind of just show you, like, how we're ingesting the data first and then how we're going to be able to use that data for both sides.
So we're bringing everything at the account level.
So this is going to be, like, deal stage changes.
We actually have a partnership API with
LinkedIn as well.
So we can bring in like impression level data.
So whether a person just sees an ad, watches a video, that type of thing.
And then we're tracking everything at the contact level.
So Dave at Lacuna Inc., he is a converted user.
We know that because we have his email address here and you can see that time at which he submitted the form.
We'll be able to see all of his touch points that led up to that form and then every touch point after, whether it's a website visit, marketing automation, email, sales touch, that type of thing.
And then we're also bringing in all the anonymous activities.
So for every anonymous user, we'll assign them a unique string of numbers and letters just to distinguish them from the next anonymous user.
But we're actually not relying on cookies for this anonymous tracking as well.

So this is a unique thing for HockeyStack is that we have a newer technology called fingerprinting where we're looking at like hundreds of different attributes about a person, their device, their location, and then using that to match it to a data set for a reverse IP lookup and match that back to the account level.
So for the, at this level, we've been able to.
See all the website activity, where they came in from, what page they were looking at, how long they were looking at it, what kind of links they clicked on, that type of thing.
And the idea is with typical attribution, you're only going to give credit to like Google Paid or maybe if you have a multi-touch model, it'll be a webinar.
But it's only going to be able to look at activity from Dave because he converted and became a lead or contact within the CRM.
But it's not going to take into account this website visit, this LinkedIn campaign that influenced the website visit, this Twitter ad, for example.

And so with our multi-touch modeling, we're going to be able to look at all of these touch points and then take that into consideration to give credit where credit is due and then use that to make decisions on what to focus on in the future.

So for Odin, you'd be able to ask like any real world question, kind of like I mentioned earlier, like eliminate the time it takes to like manually analyze reports, dashboards, that type of thing.

So what this is doing is it's pulling data from not only the reports and dashboards that do exist in your HockeyStack environment, but also every single touchpoint from every single buyer journey across every single account in order to do this analysis and give you the data that you're looking for.
So in this example, which marketing channels deliver the highest quality leads consistently?
First, Odin is going to define what it sees as a high quality lead and then break out all of these for you.
So you go to your sales qualified leads by channel, closed one revenue by channel.
And again, this is bringing in the data from the CRM, spend by channel.
And this
This bringing in the data directly from the channels themselves, so through API connections with LinkedIn, for example.
ROI, it'll break it out for you.
It'll show you all the averages, and then the insights and strategic actions and mixed recommendations.
So in this example, like, hey, shift 12% incremental budget to LinkedIn in Q3 to Safeguard Pipeline.
So this is where a lot of our customers are going to spend most of their time just looking at the data to, like, make those real-world business decisions.
And then you can even get, like, super granular.
So a question, a real-life scenario might be like, hey, do paid search leads who don't convert become more likely to convert or close faster if I were to retarget them on LinkedIn?
So typically the way to, like, get this answer is a huge process of, like, creating cohorts, doing calculations, understanding the difference between the performance of the cohorts.
And so Odin would be able to answer that in just a couple seconds here.
So what it's going to do is create those cohorts.
You have your paid search leads that had LinkedIn retargeting, and then your paid search leads that did not have LinkedIn retargeting.
It'll show the conversion rate on those.
It'll do the same calculation for average days to close.
And then it'll provide you with, like, strategic insights.
So in this scenario, yes, LinkedIn retargeting did improve outcomes, as you can see, with the conversion rate being nearly double.
And it was a shorter sales cycle.
So this is where folks are going to come in and, like, you know, really use this to make those decisions.

Like, maybe ask a real-life scenario that they're kind of envisioning or thinking about, and then just getting validation that, like, hey, the past data does prove that this is going to be a good decision.
So we're going to make that decision.
Yep.

All these columns are going to be, like, based on what they define as, like, what they want to track.
So, like, deals created might be, like, stage one opportunities, for example, whatever they call it internally.
And right now we're looking at everything through a linear model, but we can just change the attribution model here as well.
So we have last touch, first touch, a bunch of different multi-touch models.

A lot of customers like to use the predictive model, which is essentially doing, like, incrementality testing in the back end to assign different weighting to different touch points based on the impact they've had on past.
And so if you wanted to like drill into a specific channel, let's say LinkedIn ads, for example, you could click into that channel and it'll break it out by campaign.

And then you could even drill into every deal, see all the touchpoints, all the different campaigns that made an impact on that deal and all the attribution credit that gets given to every touchpoint there.

because obviously a lot of marketers are going to want to understand like how campaigns are performing month over month, quarter over quarter, that type of thing.
